Sometimes, the Play Store app's cache gets confused and checks the wrong account for your purchase license, hiding your Premium status.

To fix this, please follow these steps using the Google Play website:

1. Uninstall our app from your phone.

2. Open a web browser on a computer and go to play.google.com.
(Note: If you are using your phone's browser, you must enable "Desktop site" mode in the browser menu so it doesn't automatically redirect you to the Play Store app).

3. On the webpage, ensure you are logged in strictly with the exact Google account you used to make the purchase.

4. Search for our app and click the "Install" or "Installed" button.

5. Select your current phone from the drop-down list of devices to push the installation directly to your phone.

6. Once it installs on your phone, open the app. It should now properly verify the license against your correct account.

Please give this a try and let us know if it successfully restores your Premium features!

Best regards,
XFolder Team

templates.text:
Hi there,

Thank you for reaching out to us.

The issue you are experiencing is caused by a known glitch in the Google Play Store when handling multiple accounts on a single device. Sometimes, the Play Store app's cache gets confused and checks the wrong account for your purchase license, hiding your Premium status.

To fix this, we need to force the Play Store app to forget its current multi-account memory and recognize your purchasing account. Please follow these steps:

1. Uninstall our app from your phone.

2. Go to your device Settings > Accounts (or "Passwords & accounts"), and temporarily remove all Google accounts EXCEPT the primary one you used to make the premium purchase.

3. Next, we need to reset the store's cache. Go to your device Settings > Apps > Google Play Store > Storage, then tap Clear Data and Clear Cache. (Note: This is completely safe and won't delete any of your installed apps or personal files).

5. Open the Google Play Store app, ensure your correct account is active, and reinstall our app.

6. Open our app and verify that your Premium features are successfully restored.

7. Finally, you can safely re-add your other Google accounts back to your device settings. The app will now remain correctly tied to your premium account.

Please give this a try and let us know if it solves the problem!

Best regards,
XFolder Team

templates.category: 解释为什么m3u8不能转换为mp4
templates.text: M3U8 文件实际上是一个播放列表文件，通常用于流媒体视频的播放，而不是直接包含视频内容的文件。这种文件包含了指向视频流的链接和其他信息。因此，无法直接将 M3U8 文件转换为 MP4 或 AVI 格式，因为它们本身并不包含实际的视频数据。
